brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +45.8% since Feb 27 — rebounded 3-4% after Mon crash as Iran denied talks; Hormuz still closed; 12+ mines confirmed
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 24 (Tue): Rebounded ~3-4% to ~$103-104 after Iran denied Trump's peace talk claims, erasing Monday's crash. Mar 23 saw largest single-day swing of war ($112→$97→$104). Strait still functionally closed (~95-97% traffic reduction). 12+ Iranian mines confirmed (CBS). Goldman Sachs March-April avg forecast $110; warns could exceed $147. IEA: 'largest supply disruption in history.' Source: ICE Futures Europe / CNBC / CNN.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +37.6% since Feb 27 — rebounded 3-4% to ~$91-92; oil market remains volatile on diplomacy signals
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 24 (Tue): Rebounded ~3-4% to ~$91-92 after Iran FM Araghchi denied talks, saying 'we don't ask for ceasefire.' Previous session crashed 12.8% to $85.66 before recovering. Gas at $3.956/gal national average. Goldman raised March-April Brent avg to $110. Source: NYMEX / CNBC / CNN.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). AAA Mar 24: $3.956/gal (+32.9% since war began). Up $0.24 from a week ago. Up ~$1.02 from a month ago. California $5.65/gal (highest). Oklahoma $3.25/gal (lowest). Wholesale gasoline futures fell 9% Monday on Trump pause, but retail prices lag wholesale by days. Source: AAA / CNBC.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -309 pts / -4.5% — roughly flat Tue after Mon's +1.7% rally; volatile on Iran denial of talks; defense -1.5%; energy outperforms
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Mar 24 (Tue): ~6,559-6,588 range, roughly flat to -0.3% on day. Iran's denial of any negotiations reversed some of Monday's relief rally optimism. Defense stocks fell ~1.5% on residual de-escalation hopes (LMT -1.64%, RTX -1.42%). Energy stocks outperformed with oil rebound (XOM +2.8%, CVX +1.01%). Dow ~flat at ~46,250. Source: NYSE / CNBC / Reuters.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.43B/day — 9,000+ targets — 140+ vessels destroyed — $200B supplemental pending — 5-day pause on power plants (deadline ~Mar 28)
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 25: Trump's 5-day pause on power plant strikes continues (deadline ~Mar 28). CENTCOM updated totals: 9,000+ US targets struck, 9,000+ combat flights, 140+ vessels destroyed. IAF struck 50+ targets overnight in Iran. US struck Iran-backed commander in Iraq's Anbar province. Pakistan emerging as lead mediator; Islamabad meeting being arranged. 50,000+ US troops in CENTCOM AOR. Source: CENTCOM / CBS / CSIS / WSJ.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (25 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1B/day ongoing — ~$29-35B range — $200B supplemental pending
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 25 at ~$1-1.43B/day: ~$29-35B range. No official Day 25 total released. $200B supplemental pending but faces significant Republican resistance. Harvard est: $1T+ long-term total cost incl. $600B future medical. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CNBC / Harvard.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 9,000+ US — 9,000+ sorties — 140+ vessels destroyed — ~26,000+ combined est. — IDF: 3,000+ Iran targets — 50+ overnight
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM (Mar 24): 9,000+ US targets, 9,000+ combat flights, 140+ naval vessels destroyed. IDF cumulative: 3,000+ targets in Iran (CNN). IAF struck 50+ targets overnight Mar 24. CENTCOM update via CBS News. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~26,000+. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / CBS News / CNN.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 KIA hostile + 1 health-related death in Kuwait — ~200 wounded
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 7 by enemy fire: 6 at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait (Day 2), 1 died of wounds at Prince Sultan AB (Day 9). 6 killed in KC-135 crash over Iraq (Day 13, non-hostile). 1 health-related death in Kuwait. Total: 13 hostile + 1 non-hostile = 14. ~200 wounded (vast majority minor; 180+ returned to duty). Source: CENTCOM / AP.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: ~200 total — vast majority minor — 180+ returned to duty
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. ~200 total, vast majority minor. 180+ returned to duty. 50,000+ US service members deployed to CENTCOM AOR. Source: DoD / CENTCOM.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (Hengaw est.)
iranian_killed.note: Hengaw: 5,900 (595 civ incl. 127 minors) — HRANA: 3,114 — Health Min: 1,500+ — AP: 1,500+
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — significant discrepancies across sources. Hengaw (21-day total): 5,900 killed (595 civilian incl. 127 minors). HRANA: 3,114 deaths (1,354 civ, 1,138 mil). Iran Health Ministry: 1,500+ killed, 18,551 injured. AP (Mar 23): 1,500+ in Iran. Israeli military estimate: 3,000-4,000 soldiers only. 3.9x divergence between Health Ministry and Hengaw. 210+ children killed per Health Ministry. 80,000+ civilian units damaged incl. 300+ health/emergency facilities. Source: Hengaw / HRANA / Iran Health Ministry / AP / Israeli military.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,029+ killed — 31 healthcare workers — 1M+ displaced — 125K crossed into Syria — 3 Litani bridges destroyed
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and IDF ground operations since March 2. 1,029+ killed including 31 healthcare workers. 1M+ displaced (~19% of population). 125,000+ crossed from Lebanon into Syria. 472 schools used as shelters. IDF destroyed 3 Litani River bridges — Lebanon's president calls it 'prelude to ground invasion.' Defense Min Katz ordered demolition of border villages. HRW (Mar 23): IDF operations constitute war crimes. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / OCHA / IDF / HRW.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4.2M+ across Iran, Lebanon, Afghanistan, Pakistan — 3,000+ vessels stranded — 400 in Gulf of Oman — UNHCR / OCHA
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. UNHCR: 4.2M+ internally displaced across Afghanistan, Iran, Lebanon, and Pakistan. Iran: 3.2M (UNHCR). Lebanon: 1M+ (~19% of pop). 125,000+ Lebanese crossed into Syria. 3,000+ vessels stranded (IMO). ~400 vessels backed up in Gulf of Oman (Windward). 40,000+ sailors stranded. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / IMO / Windward.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — 3,000+ vessels stranded — 40,000+ sailors — 21+ vessel attacks — 7+ seafarer deaths
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28 (Travel Tourister/Cirium). 3,000+ vessels stranded in Middle East (IMO via NPR Mar 21). 40,000+ seafarers stranded. ~150 ships anchored outside Hormuz, ~170 containerships (450,000 TEU) trapped inside Gulf. 15,000+ cruise passengers on 6+ ships. 21+ maritime attacks, 7+ seafarer fatalities. JMIC/UKMTO: CRITICAL. Insurance at 5% of vessel value. Source: IMO / IATA / Bloomberg / UKMTO.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: Iran 210+ (Health Min) + HRANA 214 + Lebanon 118+ + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — 2,100+ killed or injured — UNICEF
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. Iran Health Ministry: 210+ children killed. HRANA (Mar 23): 214 children confirmed killed in Iran. Lebanon: 118+ children (UNICEF). Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. UNICEF (Mar 17): 2,100+ children killed or injured total across region. 324 children killed (206 Iran, 118 Lebanon). Source: UNICEF / Iran Health Min / HRANA / Lebanese MoPH.
